description: Modal confirmation dialog for high-stakes actions (delete, irreversible changes). Uses role="alertdialog" so assistive tech signals the interruption more assertively than a plain dialog. AlertDialogTitle and AlertDialogDescription are wired to aria-labelledby / aria-describedby automatically.

keyboard.action: Cycles focus through the dialog's interactive elements.
Cycles focus backwards within the dialog.
Closes the dialog without taking the action.
Activates the focused button (typically Cancel or Action).

focus.notes: Focus is trapped inside the dialog while open. On close, focus returns to the element that opened the dialog. Radix handles the save / restore automatically.

announcements: The dialog is announced with its role, title, and description in order.
screenReader.notes: Because alertdialog interrupts more assertively than dialog, prefer it only for destructive or irreversible confirmations; use plain Dialog for routine flows.

contrast.text: Title, description, and button labels all meet WCAG AA on the default theme
contrast.ui: Overlay backdrop uses 80% black for clear separation from the page

motion.reducedMotion: Radix's open / close uses fade + zoom via data-[state=open]:animate-in; wire motion-reduce:animate-none in your theme if strict reduced-motion compliance matters for your audience.

consumerResponsibilities: Always render AlertDialogTitle and AlertDialogDescription; screen readers rely on them for context.
Label the destructive action button with a verb that matches the consequence ("Delete account", not just "OK").
Consider which button should receive initial focus. By default Radix focuses the first interactive element; for destructive flows, focus Cancel instead via autoFocus.
